headline: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
summary: Nvidia is trying to borrow over $25 billion in a bond sale, which will show how much investors are willing to lend to AI-related companies

headline: All the news about Anthropic s new AI fight with the White House
summary: The US government has ordered Anthropic to limit foreign access to its latest AI models, citing concerns over their capabilities and potential misuse, amid ongoing disputes with the company

headline: A Chinese rocket breaks apart dangerously close to the Starlink constellation
summary: A Chinese rocket broke apart near a group of Starlink satellites, creating a large amount of new space debris

headline: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
summary: Meta is adding an AI feature to Facebook that uses public data from its platforms to enhance user experience

headline: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
summary: SpaceX has gone public, and investors are watching closely to see how the company performs, with some expecting big gains and others more cautious

headline: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
summary: Fox is buying Roku for $22 billion to get into smart TV technology and advertising, expanding its media reach

headline: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
summary: The US government ordered Anthropic to shut down its newest AI models, citing access restrictions for foreign nationals, sparking concerns about American control over AI technology

headline: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
summary: AMD removed a memory encryption feature from its consumer CPUs, sparking controversy among users who feel they've lost a security benefit

headline: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
summary: Big Tech companies are pushing for a single federal law to regulate AI, hoping to avoid a patchwork of different state laws and rules

headline: Claude Corps
summary: A company called Claude Corps is getting attention, but details are scarce, leaving many to wonder what they actually do

headline: 20 years of Intel Macs: Why Apple switched, and why it switched again
summary: Apple's switch to Intel Macs was a significant move, but after 20 years, the company is phasing them out, marking the end of an era

headline: Good news—we have extra time before the Sun ends life on Earth
summary: New findings suggest the Sun won't make Earth uninhabitable as soon as previously thought, giving the planet a bit more time before becoming too hot for life

headline: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
summary: Cybersecurity experts oppose US restrictions on Anthropic's AI models, saying it hinders their ability to secure software and products

headline: F1 in Spain: An old-fashioned strategy fight can still be thrilling
summary: Ferrari brought many upgrades to the Spanish F1 race, hoping to boost their performance

headline: My Homelab AI Dev Platform
summary: A developer created a home lab for testing AI projects, sharing the setup and tools used for experimentation

headline: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion
summary: Salesforce buys Fin, an AI customer service platform, to boost its Agentforce tool, aiming to improve automated tasks for businesses

headline: Skydio CEO Adam Bry on why Silicon Valley shouldn’t draw red lines for drone use
summary: The CEO of Skydio, a top US drone maker, thinks Silicon Valley should be more open to drone uses, including police and military applications, and is focusing on the enterprise market

headline: Russia appears set to finally address long-term, serious space station cracks
summary: Russia is taking steps to fix cracks in its space station, resolving a long-standing issue with NASA

headline: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
summary: Indian startup Sarvam gets big investment from HCLTech, becoming a highly valued company in the AI sector

headline: Can Europe train a frontier AI model on the compute it owns?
summary: Europe considers training advanced AI using its own computing resources, aiming for independence from other regions

headline: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
summary: A company called NewCore has raised money to help give identities to AI agents, which are becoming more common in workplaces, to manage their security

headline: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
summary: A satellite used AI to independently locate a target, a first for Earth observation satellites, potentially improving their efficiency

headline: Another nine figures for an AI coding startup that hasn't shipped
summary: A two-year-old company raised a round at a valuation that raised eyebrows across the industry, on the strength of a demo and a waitlist. No public product yet. The pitch: agents that write and merge code unsupervised — review optional, the founders say.

headline: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
summary: Mass AI layoffs contrast with huge wealth gains for a small group of industry insiders, sparking concerns about inequality

headline: A faster open model lands
summary: A new open-weight model matches last year's flagships on reasoning benchmarks while running on a single consumer GPU. The lab released the weights, the training recipe, and an unusually frank list of what it still gets wrong. Independent testers are already poking holes in the benchmark claims.

headline: Apple Foundation Models
summary: Apple is working on its own foundation models, a type of artificial intelligence that can be fine-tuned for various tasks, potentially rivaling existing models

headline: A Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-Based Transformer Method for Solving the Open Shop Scheduling Problem
summary: Researchers propose a new method using deep reinforcement learning and transformers to solve complex scheduling problems in industries, aiming to improve solution quality and efficiency

headline: UP-NRPA: User Portrait based Nested Rollout Policy Adaptation for Planning with Large Language Models in Goal-oriented Dialogue Systems
summary: Researchers propose a new method to help chatbots better adapt to individual users by using real-time feedback and large language models to customize their responses

headline: History of the Muddy Children Puzzle
summary: Researchers explore the origins of the Muddy Children Puzzle, a classic logic problem, tracing its history through various publications and adaptations over two centuries

headline: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration
summary: Researchers propose a new system to manage multiple AI agents working together across different types of tasks and data, aiming to improve collaboration and task completion

headline: Hybrid Open-Ended Tri-Evolution Makes Better Deep Researcher
summary: Researchers propose a new method combining deep research and agent evolution to improve AI agents' ability to learn and adapt in open-ended environments

headline: WorkBench Revisited: Workplace Agents Two Years On
summary: Researchers re-tested workplace agents and found significant progress, with the best agent now completing 89% of tasks and making fewer harmful mistakes, but it's unclear if this improvement will translate to real-world scenarios

headline: Regulators ask the question everyone's been dodging
summary: A draft framework would require disclosure when a person is talking to an AI rather than a human. Industry response split down predictable lines: platforms warned of friction, consumer groups called it overdue. The clause everyone's fighting over is who counts when the other side is a bot.

headline: The lab teaching models to say 'I don't know'
summary: Researchers trained a model to flag its own uncertainty instead of bluffing, cutting confident-but-wrong answers by a third. The catch: it refuses more often, and users find that annoying. Honest hesitation beats fluent nonsense, the paper argues.

headline: A coding tool that ships its own changelog
summary: The latest release writes its own release notes from the diff and the linked issues, then asks a human to approve the wording. Early users say it's the first changelog they actually read. It also quietly flags breaking changes the team forgot to document.

headline: Multimodal goes small enough to run on a phone
summary: A compact model handling text, images, and audio now fits comfortably on flagship handsets, running offline. Quality dips against the big cloud versions, but for quick captioning and translation most people won't notice. Battery drain, reviewers note, is another matter entirely.

headline: Long-context claims meet a stubborn benchmark
summary: A new test feeds models a million tokens then asks about a single buried fact. Most score well near the edges and badly in the middle — the so-called 'lost in the middle' problem persists. Vendors who advertised huge context windows were quieter this week.

headline: Chip startup banks on inference, not training
summary: While most accelerator money chases training workloads, one company raised on the bet that serving models cheaply is the bigger long-term market. Their pitch leans on power efficiency rather than peak throughput. Whether hyperscalers buy versus build remains the open question.
